questions.question: Which is the world’s first country to ban all Vulture-Toxic Drugs known so far?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh has recently banned the painkiller ketoprofen used widely to treat the cattle. With this it became the first country to ban all the drugs known so far to be toxic to vultures.Over 10 years ago, the country banned the veterinary drug diclofenac. As per Ornithologists, this is a landmark step to save the small existing population of Asia’s globally threatened vultures.

questions.question: National Urban Digital Mission is launched by which ministry?
questions.explanation: The Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has launched the “National Urban Digital Mission”. The aim of the mission is to create a shared digital infrastructure for cities.The mission would create a citizen centric approach to urban governance and service delivery in cities by 2022, and across all towns by 2024.

questions.question: ‘Alpine Plant species’, which are critically endangered have been discovered in which state?
questions.explanation: A new species of the ‘alpine plant’ in Arunachal Pradesh’s Tawang district has been discovered by a team of scientists of three institutes.The new species, which was named as ‘Cremanthodium indicum’, belongs to the family of Himalayan sunflower. The species is endemic to Penga-Teng Tso Lake of Tawang district of the state.

questions.question: What is the outlay of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme approved for the pharmaceuticals and IT hardware sector?
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet has recently approved the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for the pharmaceuticals and IT hardware sectors.The PLI scheme for pharmaceuticals with an outlay of Rs 15000 crore, has a duration of nine years, till 2028-29. The Cabinet also approved the PLI Scheme for IT hardware such as laptops, tablets, all-in-one PCs and servers with an outlay of Rs 7350 crore.

questions.question: Who is the new Chairman of National Commission for Scheduled Castes (NCSC)?
questions.explanation: Vijay Sampla, former Union Minster recently took charge as the chairman of the National Commission for Scheduled Castes (NCSC), after being appointed by the President of India.He served as the Union Minister of state for Social Justice and Empowerment from 2014 to 2019. Ram Shankar Katheria was earlier the head of NCSC.

questions.question: Ministry of Jal Shakti has selected how many sites under Phase 4 of the Swachh Iconic Places (SIP) initiative?
questions.explanation: The Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation (DDWS), Ministry of Jal Shakti has announced that it has selected 12 iconic sites under Phase 4 of the Swachh Iconic Places (SIP) initiative.SIP is an initiative of Swachh Bharat Mission Grameen (SBM-G), which aims to improve the sanitation and cleanliness standards at and around the sites. Ministry of Jal Shakti coordinates with the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, Ministry of Tourism, Ministry of Culture and the State/UT governments.

questions.question: Which state approved a law to give life imprisonment to food adulterators?
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh approved a law to give life imprisonment to food adulterators, with the cabinet recently approving a Penal Law (Madhya Pradesh Amendment) Bill, 2021.In 2019, a massive rally was organised in the state against food adulteration, with the participation of several citizens of all age groups.

questions.question: The Indian Air Force (IAF) aerobatic display teams are to participate in which country’s commemorative Air show?
questions.explanation: The Indian Air Force (IAF) aerobatic display teams, Suryakiran and Sarang, along with Light Combat Aircraft Tejas, are set to participate in the three-day Air Show.The Air Show is a part of the 70th-anniversary celebrations of the Sri Lanka Air Force (SLAF). Earlier, the SKAT- Suryakiran Aerobatic team participated in the 50th anniversary of SLAF in 2001, in Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Deepak Kumar, who won the silver medal at the Strandja Memorial Tournament in Bulgaria, is associated with which sports?
questions.explanation: Indian boxer Deepak Kumar has won the silver medal at the 72nd Strandja Memorial Tournament, recently held in Sofia, Bulgaria.Deepak Kumar was a silver medal winner of the Asian Games, and a former India Open gold-medallist.

questions.question: “Sugamya Bharat App” is being launched as a part of which ministry?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

questions.explanation: The Union Ministry of Social justice and Empowerment has developed an app named “Sugamya Bharat App”. It has been developed by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), for enhancing accessibility.It is being launched along with a hand book named “Access – The Photo Digest”.
